learningGoal: How to add letter values together — it's like cracking a number code!
How to swap letters for numbers and add them up left to right

screens.title: What is B + A + G?
screens.body:
Daisy is adding up letter values in her VR practice.

**Did you know?** In letter sums, every letter in the alphabet has a secret number: **A=1, B=2, C=3...** all the way to **Z=26**.

Swap each letter for its number, then **add them up left to right**. Let's give it a go!

**B + A + G = ???**

screens.title: Swap and Add
screens.body:
Let's solve **B + A + G**. Use EJOTY (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) to quickly find letter positions.

Then add **left to right**, one step at a time.

interaction.correctFeedback: Perfect! Swap, add left to right, convert if needed. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — first swap letters for numbers, then add left to right!

steps.why: Use EJOTY as anchor points
One step at a time — no shortcuts!
That's also the letter J

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
What is **D + A + F**?

Swap the letters for numbers, then add left to right.

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! D=4, A=1, F=6. Left to right: 4 + 1 = 5, then 5 + 6 = 11. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 11. D=4, A=1, F=6. Left to right: 4 + 1 = 5, then 5 + 6 = 11. ✓

screens.title: Adding letter values — you've got it!
screens.body: Three simple steps and you'll nail any addition letter sum:

steps.text: 1. Write the number above each letter
steps.why: EJOTY: E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25 — count from nearest
First two, then add the next, and so on

steps.text: 3. Convert back if the question asks for a letter
steps.why: Use EJOTY to find the letter from the number ✓

learningGoal: How to add up longer words without losing track — keep a running total!
How to get faster at swapping letters for numbers using the EJOTY trick

screens.title: Can you total up F + I + S + H?
screens.body:
Evie is adding up a four-letter word.

This one has more letters, but don't worry — just swap each letter for its number, then **add left to right**, keeping a running total as you go.

**F + I + S + H = ???**

screens.title: Step by step addition
screens.body: For **F + I + S + H**, we need to add 4 letters. Use **EJOTY (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25)** to find each letter's number, then keep adding one at a time. Don't try to do it all in your head at once!

interaction.sentence: With longer words, keep a running ____ as you add each letter's value
interaction.correctFeedback: Yes! A running total means adding each new number to your answer so far. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — keep a running total: add each letter's value one at a time!

steps.why: Write each number above the letter
Add one at a time, left to right
Keep a running total as you go ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body: What is **P + E + N**?

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! P=16, E=5 (EJOTY!), N=14. Left to right: 16 + 5 = 21, + 14 = 35. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 35. P=16, E=5 (EJOTY!), N=14. Left to right: 16 + 5 = 21, + 14 = 35. ✓

screens.title: Longer words? No problem!
screens.body: Longer words just mean more adding practice. Keep a running total and you'll breeze through them:

steps.text: 1. Write all the numbers above the letters
steps.why: Use EJOTY (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) to find them quickly
Start your running total

steps.text: 3. Keep adding one number at a time
steps.why: Left to right — slow and steady wins! ✓

learningGoal: How to subtract letter values — same idea, just take away instead of add!
How to work left to right with subtraction (the same golden rule)

screens.title: What is J - C?
screens.body:
Daisy is solving subtraction letter sums.

Good news — subtraction letter sums work just like addition! Swap letters for numbers, then **subtract left to right**. Same method, different sign.

**J - C = ???**

screens.title: Swap and Subtract
screens.body: Let's solve **J - C**. Use EJOTY (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) to find the numbers quickly, then subtract left to right.

steps.why: Use EJOTY as anchor points
Take the second number away from the first
Done! ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
What is **O - F**?

Swap the letters, then subtract left to right.

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! O=15 (EJOTY!), F=6. Left to right: 15 - 6 = 9. That's also the letter I. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 9. O=15 (EJOTY!), F=6. Left to right: 15 - 6 = 9. That's also the letter I. ✓

screens.title: Subtraction — same skill, different sign!
screens.body: You already know how to do this. Just follow the same three steps:

steps.text: 1. Write the number above each letter
steps.why: EJOTY helps you find positions fast
First number minus the second
If the answer is a letter, use EJOTY to find it ✓

learningGoal: How to handle letter sums that mix + and - signs together
Why going strictly left to right, one step at a time, keeps you on track

screens.title: What is H + C - E?
screens.body:
Noah is solving a mixed letter sum.

When a letter sum has **both + and -**, you must work **left to right** — do one operation at a time.

**H + C - E = ???**

screens.title: One operation at a time
screens.body: For **H + C - E**, there's both + and - mixed together. **Do the first operation, get a result, then move to the next**. Always left to right!

interaction.correctFeedback: Perfect! Swap, then work left to right through each operation. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — swap first, then do one operation at a time, left to right!

steps.why: Swap letters for numbers
Do the first operation, then use that answer for the next
Step by step gets you there ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
What is **J - D + B**?

Remember: left to right, one operation at a time.

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! J=10 (EJOTY!), D=4, B=2. Left to right: 10 - 4 = 6, then 6 + 2 = 8. That's also the letter H. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 8. J=10 (EJOTY!), D=4, B=2. Left to right: 10 - 4 = 6, then 6 + 2 = 8. That's also the letter H. ✓

screens.title: Mixed operations — one step at a time!
screens.body: When a letter sum mixes + and -, just take it steady:

steps.text: 1. Write the number above each letter
steps.why: EJOTY for quick positioning
Get a result from the first two numbers

steps.text: 3. Use that result with the NEXT operation
steps.why: Keep going left to right until you're done ✓

learningGoal: How to tackle longer mixed letter sums without getting muddled
How chaining your results left to right makes even tricky sums simple

screens.title: Can you solve E + J - A - C?
screens.body:
Evie is practising a longer mixed letter sum.

This one has multiple + and - signs. Just take it **one step at a time, left to right**.

**E + J - A - C = ???**

screens.title: Chain the calculations
screens.body: For **E + J - A - C**, there are several operations to chain. **Chain your results**: do the first, use the result for the second, and keep going.

steps.why: Write them above the letters
Each result feeds into the next operation
Left to right, every time ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
What is **T - B + A - D**?

Chain your calculations left to right.

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! T=20 (EJOTY!), B=2, A=1, D=4. Left to right: 20 - 2 = 18, + 1 = 19, - 4 = 15. That's O (EJOTY!). ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 15. T=20 (EJOTY!), B=2, A=1, D=4. Left to right: 20 - 2 = 18, + 1 = 19, - 4 = 15. That's O (EJOTY!). ✓

screens.title: Chain your results like a pro!
screens.body: For multi-step sums, think of it like passing a baton — each answer feeds into the next:

steps.text: 1. Swap all letters for numbers first
steps.why: Don't try to do it mid-calculation

steps.text: 2. Do the first operation → get a result
steps.why: This becomes the start of your next step
Result + next operation = new result ✓

learningGoal: Why BODMAS (the normal maths rule) does NOT apply to letter sums — this catches loads of people!
How to avoid the number one trap in letter sum questions

screens.title: Is 14 the right answer?
screens.body:
Daisy is checking her friend's letter sum answer.

Someone answered **B + C × D = 14** using BODMAS (the order you normally do calculations: Brackets, Orders, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction).

But wait — **letter sums do NOT use BODMAS!** You must work **left to right**.

What's the real answer?

screens.title: BODMAS is WRONG here!
screens.body:
Someone said **B + C × D = 14** using BODMAS (Brackets, Orders, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction). But that's WRONG!

In maths class, you use BODMAS. But in **letter sums on the 11+**, you work **strictly LEFT to RIGHT**.

This is the **number one trap** in letter sum questions!

steps.why: This is what most people do — but it's WRONG for letter sums!

steps.text: RIGHT (Left to right): Left to right: 2 + 3 = 5, then 5 × 4 = 20 = 20
steps.why: In letter sums, ALWAYS go left to right

steps.text: The correct answer is 20 (= letter T)
steps.why: Don't let BODMAS trick you! ✓

screens.title: Your turn — avoid the trap!
screens.body:
What is **A + D × B**?

**Remember: LEFT TO RIGHT, not BODMAS!**

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! LEFT TO RIGHT (not BODMAS!): A + D = 1 + 4 = 5, then 5 × B = 5 × 2 = 10. BODMAS would wrongly give 1 + 8 = 9. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 10. LEFT TO RIGHT (not BODMAS!): A + D = 1 + 4 = 5, then 5 × B = 5 × 2 = 10. BODMAS would wrongly give 1 + 8 = 9. ✓

screens.title: The BODMAS Trap — you'll never fall for it again!
screens.body: This is the **biggest trap** in letter sums, and now you know how to beat it:

steps.why: Brackets, Orders, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction

steps.text: In LETTER SUMS → work LEFT TO RIGHT
steps.why: Forget BODMAS completely! Go strictly left to right
The BODMAS answer is ALWAYS a wrong option to trick you! ✓

learningGoal: How to train your brain to ignore BODMAS when you see letter sums
How to make left-to-right your automatic habit — so the trap never catches you

screens.title: Don't fall for the trap!
screens.body:
Evie is carefully avoiding the BODMAS trap.

**D + A × F = ???**

The question has a × sign. Your brain might want to use BODMAS (Brackets, Orders, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction) — but **DON'T!** Work left to right.

screens.title: Left to right, always!
screens.body: For **D + A × F**, your brain might want to do the × first — but DON'T! Even when you see × or ÷ in a letter sum, **always work left to right**. The BODMAS answer will be there as a trap option!

steps.why: Swap letters for numbers
Do each operation in order, left to right
The trap answer is always in the options — don't pick it! ✓

screens.title: Your turn — left to right!
screens.body:
What is **B + C × E**?

**LEFT TO RIGHT, not BODMAS!**

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! LEFT TO RIGHT: B + C = 2 + 3 = 5, then 5 × E = 5 × 5 = 25. That's Y (EJOTY!). BODMAS would wrongly give 2 + 15 = 17. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 25. LEFT TO RIGHT: B + C = 2 + 3 = 5, then 5 × E = 5 × 5 = 25. That's Y (EJOTY!). BODMAS would wrongly give 2 + 15 = 17. ✓

screens.title: BODMAS trap? Not any more!
screens.body: You've learned to spot and dodge this trap. Here's your reminder:

steps.text: 1. See × or ÷ in a letter sum? Don't panic!
steps.why: Your brain will scream 'BODMAS!' — ignore it
Do the first operation, then use the result for the next

steps.text: 3. The BODMAS answer is a trap option
steps.why: If you see it in the choices, DON'T pick it! ✓

learningGoal: How to handle multiplication in letter sums — times tables to the rescue!
How the left-to-right rule works with × signs too

screens.title: What is C × D?
screens.body:
Daisy is solving a multiplication letter sum.

Some letter sums use **multiplication (×)**. The rule is the same: swap letters for numbers, then work **left to right**.

**C × D = ???**

screens.title: Multiply left to right
screens.body:
Let's solve **C × D**. Multiplication letter sums follow the same rule: **swap and calculate, left to right**.

Important: normal maths uses **BODMAS** (Brackets, Orders, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction) — but letter sums do NOT! Always work **left to right**.

interaction.sentence: Even with multiplication, letter sums are always worked ____ to ____
interaction.correctFeedback: Yes! Left to right — the same rule applies to all operations in letter sums. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — ALL letter sum operations go left to right, including multiplication!

steps.why: EJOTY (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) helps find numbers fast
Use your times tables!
Convert back if the question asks for a letter ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
What is **B × H**?

Remember: left to right, even with multiplication.

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! B=2, H=8. Left to right: 2 × 8 = 16. That's also the letter P. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 16. B=2, H=8. Left to right: 2 × 8 = 16. That's also the letter P. ✓

screens.title: Multiplication — same rules, you've got this!
screens.body: Multiplication letter sums follow the exact same pattern you already know:

steps.why: Use EJOTY for speed
Same rule as always — left to right!

steps.text: 3. Remember: NOT BODMAS (Brackets, Orders, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction)!
steps.why: A + B × C means (A+B) × C in letter sums ✓

learningGoal: How to turn your number answer back into a letter — the final piece of the puzzle!
How EJOTY makes finding any letter from its number super quick

screens.title: F + D = which LETTER?
screens.body:
Daisy is solving a letter sum where the answer is a letter.

Here's a fun twist — sometimes the question wants the answer **as a letter**, not a number! You work out the number first, then convert it back using EJOTY.

**F + D = ?** (Give the letter!)

screens.title: Number → Letter using EJOTY
screens.body:
For **F + D**, we first calculate the number, then convert it back to a letter. Use EJOTY (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) to convert back.

Find the nearest EJOTY letter and count up or down.

steps.why: First get the number answer
EJOTY letters are your anchor points
Number → letter — done! ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
What letter does **F + D** give you?

Calculate the number, then convert to a letter using EJOTY.

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! F=6, D=4. Left to right: 6 + 4 = 10. Convert: 10 = J (from EJOTY — J is the 10th letter). ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is J. F=6, D=4. Left to right: 6 + 4 = 10. Convert: 10 = J (from EJOTY — J is the 10th letter). ✓

screens.title: Number to letter — easy with EJOTY!
screens.body: Converting numbers back to letters is a doddle once you know the trick:

steps.why: Swap and calculate as normal

steps.text: 3. Count up or down from EJOTY

learningGoal: How to use EJOTY (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) as your speed hack for any letter
How counting from the nearest EJOTY letter saves you loads of time

screens.title: What number is the letter H?
screens.body:
Daisy is using EJOTY to find letter positions quickly.

Counting A, B, C, D... all the way to H would take ages! But here's a brilliant shortcut: use **EJOTY** (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) as anchor points.

Just find the nearest one and count from there — so much faster!

screens.title: Count from the nearest EJOTY
screens.body: To find **H**, start at the nearest EJOTY letter (**J=10**) and count **back**.

steps.why: Every 5th letter is an EJOTY anchor
Answer: 12 (= L) ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
What is **H + D**?

Use EJOTY to find the letter positions quickly!

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! H=8 (J=10, count back 2), D=4. Left to right: 8 + 4 = 12. That's L. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 12. H=8 (J=10, count back 2), D=4. Left to right: 8 + 4 = 12. That's L. ✓

screens.title: EJOTY — your secret weapon!
screens.body: EJOTY gives you 5 anchor points in the alphabet:

steps.why: Memorise these — they're always the same!

steps.text: Find the nearest anchor, count up or down
steps.why: H? J=10, count back 2 → H=8
Got 18? T=20, count back 2 → R ✓

learningGoal: How to handle letters that sit between two EJOTY anchors
How to get lightning-fast at converting letters to numbers

screens.title: How fast can you find L?
screens.body:
Evie is finding a letter that's between two EJOTY points.

The letter **L** — which EJOTY (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) anchor is nearest?

screens.title: Count from the anchor
screens.body: Start at **J=10** and count **forward** to reach **L**.

steps.why: Pick the closest anchor
So L = 12

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
What is **L + C**?

Use EJOTY to find L's value, then calculate.

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! L=12 (J=10, count forward 2), C=3. Left to right: 12 + 3 = 15. That's O (EJOTY!). ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 15. L=12 (J=10, count forward 2), C=3. Left to right: 12 + 3 = 15. That's O (EJOTY!). ✓

screens.title: EJOTY master — that's you!
screens.body: With practice, you'll find letter positions in seconds. Here are your cheat notes:

steps.text: Letters near E(5): A=1, B=2, C=3, D=4, F=6, G=7
steps.why: Count from E for these

steps.text: Letters near J(10) or O(15): H, I, K, L, M, N
steps.why: Count from J or O — whichever is closer

steps.text: Letters near T(20) or Y(25): R, S, U, V, W, X, Z
steps.why: Z=26 is just 1 after Y! ✓

learningGoal: How to handle the bigger numbers that come with letters near the end of the alphabet
How to stay accurate when the numbers get larger — slow and steady!

screens.title: What is W + X?
screens.body:
Daisy is working with big letter values.

Letters near the end of the alphabet have **big numbers**: T=20, U=21, V=22, W=23, X=24, Y=25, Z=26.

Use EJOTY (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) anchors to find them quickly!

**W + X = ???**

screens.title: Big letters, same method
screens.body:
For **W + X**, we're working with big letter values. The method is exactly the same — just take extra care with the arithmetic because the numbers are larger!

Use **T=20** and **Y=25** as your anchors.

steps.why: Use T=20 and Y=25 to find these quickly
Same method — just bigger numbers
Take your time with the arithmetic ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
What is **W + X**?

Remember: T=20, Y=25 — count from there for end-of-alphabet letters.

interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! W=23 (Y=25, count back 2), X=24 (Y=25, count back 1). Left to right: 23 + 24 = 47. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is 47. W=23 (Y=25, count back 2), X=24 (Y=25, count back 1). Left to right: 23 + 24 = 47. ✓

screens.title: Big letters, no problem!
screens.body: Letters near the end of the alphabet just need a bit more care. You've got this:

steps.why: Know these — they come up a LOT

steps.text: Use T=20 and Y=25 as anchors
steps.why: Count forward from T or backward from Y

steps.text: Take extra care with bigger numbers
steps.why: Write your working down to avoid mistakes ✓
